originalTitle: Seven goals, fastest strike and two red cards - Bayern-Real delivers drama
originalDescription: The quickest strike of this season's Champions League, seven goals, two red cards and the 15-time winners exiting in anger - once again the knockout stages of Europe's elite tournament delivered drama in spades.

originalTitle: Arsenal limp to semis but Arteta wanted fire - does style matter?
originalDescription: Mikel Arteta wanted "fire" from his Arsenal players but instead got a sluggish performance as the Gunners did what was needed to reach the Champions League semi-finals.

originalTitle: Play-off pending? How a draw at the Etihad could set up thrilling finale
originalDescription: Man City's meeting against Arsenal may go a long way to deciding the destination of the Premier League trophy in May, but how close a finish could it be?

originalTitle: £124m spent but Newcastle no closer to striker solution
originalDescription: Newcastle United spent £124m to replace departing Alexander Isak, but Eddie Howe's men now seem no closer to finding their striking solution.

originalTitle: Liverpool lead Senesi race - Thursday's gossip
originalDescription: Bournemouth's Marcos Senesi on Liverpool's radar, Reds also interested in Newcastle forward Anthony Gordon, while Manchester City are tracking Feyenoord defender Givairo Read.

originalTitle: How qualifying for Champions League could affect Man Utd's loanees
originalDescription: Man Utd want to qualify for the Champions League but if they do it will make Andre Onana and Marcus Rashford harder to sell.

originalTitle: Real Madrid set for rare trophyless season - will Arbeloa pay the price?
originalDescription: After losing out to Bayern Munich in a thrilling Champions League quarter-final, Real Madrid face the prospect of a trophyless season that could cost Alvaro Arbeloa his job.

originalTitle: Maguire gets extra ban and will miss Chelsea trip
originalDescription: Manchester United defender Harry Maguire will miss Saturday's trip to Chelsea after receiving an additional one-match ban.
We have a busy day’s racing on Thursday with action from Hereford and Ripon, live on Sky Sports Racing...

originalTitle: LIV Golf season to continue 'full throttle' amid shutdown reports, says CEO
originalDescription: LIV Golf CEO Scott O'Neil sought to quell speculation about the league's financial future with a memo to his staff that said the 2026 season will continue as planned without interruption and "at full throttle".

originalTitle: 'They're in different time zones' | Is this the most bizarre misfield you've ever seen?
originalDescription: Lucknow Super Giants have mix-up in the field as Abdul Samad tries to keep the ball in play near the boundary in an attempt to direct the ball towards Aiden Markram to take the catch and the opportunity goes missing.

originalTitle: Explained: Why Saudi investment in LIV Golf might be coming to an end
originalDescription: Sky Sports News chief correspondent Kaveh Solhekol reveals Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund might be reconsidering its financial support to LIV Golf.

originalTitle: 'They need a big-name signing!' - Red Bull urged to stop 'brain drain'
originalDescription: Red Bull need to make a "big-name signing" from elsewhere on the Formula 1 grid to start turning the tide of rivals hiring their senior figures, says Sky Sports F1's Karun Chandhok.

originalTitle: Relive LIV Golf's tumultuous past as speculation mounts over its future
originalDescription: With speculation suggesting Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund might be withdrawing their financial support to LIV Golf, relive the organisation's rocky relationship with the rest of the golfing world.

originalTitle: McGinley: PGA Tour needs to reward players who stayed loyal
originalDescription: With LIV Golf's future in doubt, Paul McGinley believes the PGA Tour needs to reward the players who stayed loyal to them.

originalTitle: Greaves has shifted darts beyond tired 'Battle of the Sexes' narrative
originalDescription: When Beau Greaves produced a stunning win over world No 1 Luke Littler at the PDC World Youth Championships last October it should have put her name in lights.
